findings.title: EU-US tariff regime escalates to 15% fixed rate under Turnberry deal
findings.summary: EU Parliament endorsed Turnberry deal fixing US tariffs on EU exports at 15%, shifting drivers from uncertainty to competitiveness loss. EU export growth to US projected to slow 4.6% in 2026 due to higher tariffs and eroding relative position vs China/India. This locks in structural trade friction amid active protocol.
findings.claim_excerpt: The Turnberry deal locks in a 15% tariff rate for EU exports... EU export growth to the US is expected to slow further by around 4.6% in 2026 due to tariffs alone.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term trade drag from fixed higher tariffs outweighs uncertainty reduction, per protocol upgrade for trade volume indicators.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Elevates EU-US decoupling, pressuring EM exporters and metals via input costs; bonds face inflation pass-through risks.
findings.confidence_basis: Multiple sources confirm Turnberry endorsement and 15% rate impact on EU exports.

sources_cited.excerpt: Turnberry deal locks in a 15% tariff rate for EU exports
2025-2026 reciprocal tariff regime has introduced a new framework that further escalates duties

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Corroborated by financial press and consulting analysis on fixed 15% rate and export slowdown.

cross_monitor_relevance.esa: Accelerates EU fiscal autonomy push via trade coercion
cross_monitor_relevance.scem: EM competitiveness erosion vs EU in US market

findings.review_reason: Assess ratification progress and weekly export data divergence

findings.title: Section 232 steel/aluminum tariffs remain at 25%/10% on EU imports
findings.summary: EU steel and aluminum face ongoing 25% and 10% US tariffs under Section 232, hitting automotive and construction sectors. This sustains inflationary pressures on metals supply chains amid reciprocal regime. Manufacturers report elevated input costs with no de-escalation.
findings.claim_excerpt: EU steel and aluminum products face 25% and 10% tariffs respectively under Section 232.
findings.signal_type_basis: Regime-level input cost inflation embedded in tariff shock protocol for metals.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Structural cost push to manufacturing and real estate construction materials.
findings.confidence_basis: Directly stated in multiple trade analyses as active policy.

sources_cited.excerpt: Steel and Aluminum: EU steel and aluminum products face 25% and 10% tariffs

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Consistent across consulting reports as ongoing regime.

cross_monitor_relevance.scem: Commodity input stress for EM manufacturers

findings.review_reason: Track negotiation progress on Section 232 disputes

findings.title: US launches probe into EU industrial overcapacity, EC denies claims
findings.summary: US opened new investigations into EU exports for unfair overcapacity practices, prompting EC rejection. Follows Supreme Court ruling invalidating prior tariffs, with 10% duties reimposed on top of 4.8%. Heightens trade uncertainty in cyclical sectors.
findings.claim_excerpt: Washington launched fresh trade investigations targeting the EU... imposed new tariffs of 10% following the court ruling.
findings.signal_type_basis: Cyclical probe escalates conditional trade barriers, protocol upgrades export-sensitive signals.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Risks further hardware/tech exposure and EM diversion flows.
findings.confidence_basis: Reported by press with legal context from court ruling.

sources_cited.excerpt: US opens new probe... Commission denies unfair trade practices
EU faces a complex patchwork of US tariffs

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Cross-confirmed probe launch and tariff reimposition.

findings.episodic_reason: Recent probe, monitor for enactment 3+ weeks
findings.review_reason: Watch for probe outcomes and retaliation filings

findings.title: USMCA 2026 review negotiations heighten North America supply chain uncertainty
findings.summary: US, Canada, Mexico prepare USMCA review focusing on rules of origin, energy policies, and Section 232 tariffs. Failure to renew risks exposing partners to US tariffs; submissions due June 1. Impacts auto and energy sectors amid tariff regime.
findings.claim_excerpt: USMCA review in 2026 creating investment uncertainty... Governments submit changes by June 1st.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term trend in regional trade rules amid active tariff protocol.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Potential escalation for energy exporters and metals via stricter origins.
findings.confidence_basis: Single primary source on timeline, corroborated by USTR agenda.

sources_cited.excerpt: USMCA statutory review... submissions by June 1st
Conduct the Review of the United States–Mexico–Canada Agreement

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: USTR confirms review intent; consulting details timeline.

findings.review_reason: Monitor submission deadlines and reform proposals

findings.title: EU business insolvencies elevated due to tariff-driven input costs
findings.summary: Persistent insolvencies in EU manufacturing from high input costs and tariff disruptions, expected above pre-pandemic levels into H2 2026. Coface forecasts improvement only post-rate cuts. Secondary real estate pressure via construction.
findings.claim_excerpt: Business insolvencies expected to remain above pre-pandemic levels... hit by high input costs and supply chain disruptions due to tariff policies.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term sovereign stress from tariff costs, not episodic.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Signals lagged real estate weakness and staples margin pressure.
findings.confidence_basis: Consulting report citing Coface, aligned with tariff exposure.

sources_cited.excerpt: Manufacturers hit hard by high input costs... tariff policies

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Direct link to tariffs; needs additional Coface primary for Confirmed.

findings.title: USTR 2026 Agenda doubles down on America First tariffs and reciprocity
findings.summary: USTR outlines 2026 policy prioritizing tariff enforcement, USMCA review, and China reciprocity. Notes US trade deficit with EU exceeds China; commits to monitoring evasion. Reinforces tariff shock as core regime.
findings.claim_excerpt: Trump Administration is going to double down on the America First Trade Policy in 2026... U.S. goods trade deficit with EU ($218.8B).
findings.signal_type_basis: Regime-level policy confirmation under active protocol.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Cements structural decoupling, dollar strength embedded.
findings.confidence_basis: Official USTR document, corroborated by press.

sources_cited.excerpt: Double down on America First Trade Policy in 2026

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Tier 1 primary source with press alignment.
